Israeli air defenses, aided by the United States, United Kingdom, France, Jordan, and Bahrain, intercepted the vast majority of the incoming projectiles. Israeli officials reported limited damage and no casualties, although some minor injuries were sustained. The IRGC’s attack was preceded by a public announcement from Iran indicating its intent to retaliate for the Damascus strike. This announcement served as a warning to Israel and the international community, allowing them time to prepare for the response. The timing of the attack, occurring on a Saturday night in Israel, likely aimed to maximize impact and minimize civilian casualties. The Damascus airstrike, attributed by Western media to Israel, significantly escalated tensions between Iran and Israel. The IRGC officers killed were reportedly involved in coordinating Iranian support for Hezbollah and other proxy groups operating in Lebanon and Syria. Iran has consistently sought to expand its influence in the region through these proxy forces, challenging U.S. and Israeli interests. Following the missile attacks, Iran has signaled a willingness to engage in diplomatic efforts to de-escalate the situation, although it has also warned of further action if Israel responds. The international community is now focused on preventing further escalation and establishing a sustainable path towards de-escalation. The attack raises concerns about a wider regional conflict. The intervention in the war in Yemen (which began in 2015) marked a turning point, demonstrating a willingness to assert itself as a strategic actor in the Persian Gulf and the Middle East. The Rise to Power of Mohammed bin Zayed MBZ holds the position of Supreme Commander of the UAE Armed Forces. In 2004 he was appointed Crown Prince, and his subsequent appointment as President the day after his father’s death was predictable, as a demonstration of unity and political continuity in the country. Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan had, in reality, been de facto ruler since 2014, the year his father suffered a stroke, and in 2019 the New York Times had already bestowed upon him the title of the world’s most powerful Arab leader. The Expansion of Emirati Influence In recent years, the UAE has deployed various means – diplomatic, military, and economic – to expand its influence.
